channel.title: Corpus Linguistics and Linguistic Theory
channel.description: A RSS feed containing the latest articles published in Corpus Linguistics and Linguistic Theory

item.title: I couldn’t help but wonder: do modals and negation attract?
item.description: Article by Qiaoyun Chen DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/cllt-2023-0029

item.title: Reliable detection and quantification of selective forces in language change
item.description: Article by Qiaoyun Chen DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/cllt-2023-0064

item.title: Alternation in the Mandarin disposal constructions: quantifying their evolutionary dynamics across twelve centuries
item.description: Article by Qiaoyun Chen DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/cllt-2023-0038

item.title: Critical contingency competition in L2 clause positioning acquisition: the case of concessive clause by Chinese EFL learners
item.description: Article by Qiaoyun Chen DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/cllt-2023-0068

item.title: The distributional properties of long nominal compounds in scientific articles: an investigation based on the uniform information density hypothesis
item.description: Article by Qiaoyun Chen DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/cllt-2023-0028

item.title: The counting principle makes number words unique
item.description: Article by Qiaoyun Chen DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/cllt-2023-0105

item.title: Profiling analytic causative construction in Chinese: a multifactorial analysis of diachronic change
item.description: Article by Qiaoyun Chen DOI: https://doi.org/10.1515/cllt-2023-0062
